title: mZN Staining Protocol
author: Asar Khan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.r4xd8xn

steps.instruction: Spread the stool sample evenly on the middle of the slide with constant rotational movement. 00:10:00, (5 to 10 minutes) for rotational movement undetermined, (Amount of stool sample)
Place the slides on dryer with smeared surface upwards and air-dried it. Undetermined 00:10:00, minutes
Fix the dried smear with absolute methanol. 00:05:00, or (3-5 minutes)
Now, add the Carbol-fuchsine solution to the slide to cover the whole smear. Carbol-Fuchsin Contributed by users 00:20:00, minutes
Wash the slide gently with tap water with the help of a dropper. Do not expose the slides to the high pressure of tap water directly, rather it will be better to use a dropper for washing the slides.
After washing the slide, add decolorizer (Acid Alcohol) to the smear and wash the slide again with tap water. Acid Alcohol Contributed by users undetermined, or 4-6 drops
Now, add the counter stain (Methylene Blue) and wash the slide with clean water. Methylene Blue Gold Biotechnology Catalog #M-680 00:05:00, minutes wait for methylene blue

Than examine the smear with the help of a compound microscope with 40x and 100x (immersion oil lens) objective and scan the smear throughly for parasite identification. (Olympus, CH20i, Biological microscope , Anti-fungus treated optics , Built to last- Superior build quality, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/4jg2t76.jpg )
Clean the back side of the slide with a tissue paper and put it in the draining rack to air dry. 00:05:00, minutes, wait for slide to dry
